metadata.title: Build a Risk-Tiered Verification Checklist with Claude
metadata.note: CLI video: 10-beat spine B00–B09. B04 is slate. No audio.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI, then take it apart.
Reading Claude's output is not reviewing it. The verification matrix turns 'looks right' into a checkable protocol. The human gate is not distrust — it is the design of your supervisory role.

shot.notes: hook card: 'Reading is not reviewing.' — CRIMSON on 'reading', teal on 'verification protocol' — the gate framing

beats.narration_text: Build verification_gate.py: takes output type and risk level as arguments, prints a tailored verification checklist — three to five concrete checkable steps per combination.

props.command:
claude "Build verification_gate.py:
  args: --output-type and --risk-level
  output types: summary citation number
               chart code recommendation
  risk levels: light moderate strict
  print 3-5 concrete checkable steps
  for each combination"

beats.narration_text: Check the code. Citation always includes 'open the source' as a required step. Strict risk always includes independent recalculation for numbers. Chart output includes axis-label and denominator checks. Each combination has its own rule — none is generic.

Run it on three combinations: citation at strict — four steps including open the source. Number at moderate — spot-check, denominator, units. Code at light — skim for obvious errors. Each checklist is tailored and checkable.

shot.notes: terminal: three verification_gate.py runs — citation strict (4 steps), number moderate (3 steps), code light (2 steps) — each checklist printed cleanly

beats.narration_text: Add a log flag that exports the completed checklist with timestamps as a markdown file — the verification record that travels with the output.

props.command:
claude "update verification_gate.py:
  add --log flag
  exports checklist with ISO timestamps
  as markdown file: verification-log.md
  for audit trail purposes"

beats.narration_text: Run with the log flag. The markdown file writes: output type, risk level, timestamp, and each step with a checkbox. The verification record is now an artifact — it travels with the output, not just the output itself.

shot.notes: terminal: --log flag run, then cat verification-log.md — markdown table with ISO timestamps, checkbox items, teal header

beats.narration_text: The human gate is not distrust — it is the design of your supervisory role. The checklist makes that role automatic rather than effortful. Without it, 'looks right' is your entire review process.

shot.notes: teardown card: 'Looks right → no protocol → unsupervisable' (CRIMSON) vs 'Checklist → automatic supervisory role → auditable' (teal)

beats.narration_text: Your move: run your next Claude output through the matrix and log the checklist. Next reel: the privacy classification scanner — the folder you think is safe probably is not.

shot.notes: next-reel teaser: 'Next: Privacy Classification Scanner' — segment 07 of the CLAUDE series

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI. Then take it apart. At Nik Bear Brown, nikbearbrown dot com.
